steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: 'The book wasn't written solely by Legler' and 'other authors, such as Stephen Graham Jones and Barry Lopez, also contributed essays,' with 'however' marking the contrast. Two valid placements exist for the conjunctive adverb: ending IC1 as a trailing parenthetical (', however;') or starting IC2 ('; however,'). Test which placement the sentence prefers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Legler, however,: Comma before and comma after 'however' joins two independent clauses with only commas — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — Legler; however,: Grammatically valid, but placing 'however' at the start of IC2 shifts the emphasis to the list of contributors as the surprising claim, rather than qualifying the sole-authorship assertion. Less idiomatic for this sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — Legler. However,: Splits one continuous contrast into two sentences, misrepresenting the passage's unified structure. ✗
  • Choice D — Legler, however;: The comma binds 'however' to IC1 as a trailing parenthetical, qualifying the claim that the book was written solely by Legler. The semicolon then joins IC2 about the other contributors. Parse: [IC1 + trailing 'however']; [IC2]. The contrast attaches directly to the sole-authorship assertion — the most natural semantic fit. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
